notes: Iteration 2 vs iteration 1 with-skill: tokens dropped 33% (104k→69k avg), tool calls dropped 30% (38→27 avg). The baked-in naming convention table eliminated organic discovery overhead.
Eval 1 now surfaces Linear migration issue SW2CAT-1891 and DRI Eric Aiello — both missed in iteration 1. The structured step sequence guides the agent to check Linear.
Eval 2 now produces a 'Do not run this query' safety verdict with column name corrections and DSL3 flag — iteration 1 baseline had 33% pass rate on this eval; iteration 2 skill hits 100%.
Eval 3 (schema owner): still failing the Slack assertion due to expired xoxp token — not a skill quality issue. All substantive content improved: named DRIs, go/selectstar, Google Sheets column docs.
Recurring blocker: Slack xoxp token is expired in all with-skill iteration-2 runs. This needs a token refresh to unlock the full Slack research capability.
The skill's 'Related Tables' section is now consistently populated. The ecosystem appendix gives users immediate context on real production alternatives.
Efficiency gain is significant: iteration-2 with-skill outperforms iteration-1 baseline on both quality (pass rate) and efficiency (tokens, tool calls, time).
